mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5d returns during recovery periods, with mean forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 245 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 245 consistently coincide with positive 60d forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20d returns, with a negative mean return in triggered states.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical relationship with 5d forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces corporate profit expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 60d returns during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.
